sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.88 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: An undirected graph is called a locally finite Cayley graph of a group\/~$G$if its vertex set can be identified with the set of elements of$G$in such a way that, for some finite generating set$X=X^{-1}$of$G$not containing~$1$, two vertices$g$and$h$are adjacent if and only if$g^{-1}h X$. Do there exist two finitely generated groups with the same locally finite Cayley graph, one of which is periodic...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
